fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Alaska Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Alaska - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity alaska, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to alaska, pharmacy technician compact alaska, alaska pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Alaska Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: Division of Corporations, Business and Professional Licensing
board.mailing_address:
State of Alaska
Board of Pharmacy
P.O. Box 110806
Juneau, AK 99811-0806

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Alaska.

steps.title: 1. Create an Account and Start Application
steps.description: Go to the ProfessionalLicense.Alaska.Gov portal (professionallicense.alaska.gov), create a new user account if you don't have one, then select 'Apply for a License' and choose 'Pharmacy Technician - Initial' from the list of professions.

steps.title: 2. Complete Application and Pay Fees
steps.description: Fill out all sections of the online application form accurately. You will need to pay the non-refundable initial application fee ($50) and the initial license fee ($100) through the portal. The total fee is $150.

steps.title: 3. Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Upload or arrange for the submission of official transcripts verifying your high school diploma or GED. Ensure that the Board can verify your current national certification from either the Pharmacy Technician Certification Board (PTCB) or the National Healthcareer Association (NHA).

steps.title: 4. Complete a Criminal Background Check (CBC)
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within the application or by the Board to complete a Criminal Background Check (CBC). This typically involves submitting fingerprints and an authorization form as required by the State of Alaska.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the PTCB. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Alaska pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Alaska have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Alaska. To work in Alaska, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Alaska?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Alaska while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Alaska
hero.dek: Apply online through Alaska's ProfessionalLicense.Alaska.Gov portal (professionallicense.alaska.gov). Create an account, search for 'Pharmacy Technician - Initial', and submit your application along with all required documentation and a criminal background check.
